step_by_step_reasoning: The transcript and whiteboard layout describe a contact center solution called ContactSuite. I'll represent the flows as: 1) Voice/Chat Ingestion via Connect, processed by a routing Lambda, integrated with ECS microservices to CRMs, and popped onto ContactSuite's agent screen; 2) Email Ingestion via SES and a dedicated processing Lambda; 3) Data persistence to DynamoDB and RDS for state and reporting. We represent the ContactSuite core hosted on ELB/EC2/ASG as an EC2 node.

graph.name: ContactSuite Contact Center Scale Architecture
graph.notes: Integrates AWS Connect, SES, and local custom microservices to empower support agents with smart screen pops and automated CRM data retrieval.

nodes.name: Caller Carrie
nodes.notes: Initiates voice calls, chats, or emails.

nodes.name: Amazon Connect
nodes.notes: Handles contact flows, IVR, and chat/voice channels.

nodes.name: Contact Flow Lambda
nodes.notes: Determines call routing logic and automation triggers.

nodes.name: CRM Integration Microservices
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Handles CRM backend queries during active sessions.

nodes.name: External CRMs
nodes.notes: CRM platforms such as Salesforce or Siebel.

nodes.name: ContactSuite Engine
nodes.notes: Hosted on ELB, EC2, and ASG. Embeds SenseIQ KB and serves agent screens.

nodes.name: Agent Andy
nodes.notes: Receives real-time browser screen pops and calls.

nodes.name: Email Handler Lambda
nodes.notes: Processes and normalizes incoming emails for queue injection.

nodes.name: DynamoDB
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores fast transactional, session, and state records.

nodes.name: RDS Database
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Relational store used for reporting and analytics.

edges.notes: Caller initiates voice or chat session
Pops customized data form and SenseIQ KB to agent screen
Sends email to contact center support
Presents processed email as standard chat/voice work unit
Saves operational metrics and session data
Saves historical configuration and reporting metrics
